title: Corrective feedback
body: Corrective feedback is a frequently-used practice in the field of learning and achievement. It typically involves a learner receiving either formal or informal feedback on their understanding or performance on various tasks by an agent such as teacher, coach, employer or peer(s). To successfully deliver corrective feedback, it needs to be nonevaluative, supportive, timely, and specific.
categories: Articles needing additional references from May 2021
